title: Goldman in talks to offload Apple credit, savings products to American Express, source says
text: Goldman Sachs is in talks to offload its Apple credit card and high-yield savings account products to American Express, a source told CNBC's Leslie Picker.

title: Dow Jones Today: Banks Bump Up Index
text: JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs shares gained ground after passing the Fed's stress test, lifting other financials with them.

title: Joonko seemingly lied about working with American Express, PayPal, Accenture
text: Joonko's board has formally accused ousted CEO Ilit Raz of exaggerating the size of its business.

title: American Express: A Bad Investment As Marketing Push Flops
text: American Express, in a push to grow its market share, made an aggressive card revamp and advertising push to its top cards. While it made some noise and garnered interest, it also increased the company's expenses and as a result, it is projected to underperform peers when it comes to revenues and profits. Due to these facts, I believe AXP makes for an inferior investment in the financial industry, even within the growing credit card market.

title: American Express Names New CFO as Jeffrey Campbell Announces Retirement
text: After a decade with the company, American Express Chief Financial Officer Jeffrey Campbell is set to retire. The company announced Campbell's departure in a Tuesday (June 27) press release, saying he would be succeeded by Deputy CFO Christophe Le Caillec.

title: American Express Announces Retirement of Chief Financial Officer Jeffrey C. Campbell and Names Deputy Chief Financial Officer Christophe Le Caillec as Successor
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--American Express Company (NYSE:AXP) today announced that Jeffrey C. Campbell, Vice Chairman and Chief Financial Officer, has decided to retire from the company. He will step down as the company's CFO as of August 14, 2023, at which time Christophe Le Caillec, Deputy Chief Financial Officer, will succeed him as CFO, and join the American Express Executive Committee. Mr. Campbell will remain with American Express as Vice Chairman until March 2024. “On behalf of the Boar.

title: Where Will American Express Be in 1 Year?
text: American Express has succeeded at recruiting a younger prosperous client base. It's feeling economic pressure in its net income, as it has built up provisions for losses.

title: American Express operational risk from new international regulations ‘overstated,' analysts say
text: The operational risk facing American Express Company (NYSE:AXP) from the forthcoming Basel 3 endgame/Basel 4 proposal (B3/B4), being efforts by regulators to amend certain aspects of large banks' capital rules, has been overstated according to analysts at UBS. “Calculation of operational risk-weighted assets (RWAs) under the Basel proposal is especially punitive for firms with greater size and fee income streams,” the analysts explained in a note to clients.

title: FDIC studying plan to include smaller U.S. banks in Basel III capital requirements after failures in early 2023
text: The chairman of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. said Thursday that U.S. regulators are considering a plan to include banks with assets as low as $100 billion in the international regime for capital requirements known as Basel III.

title: PayEm Integrates Spend Management and Procurement Platform With American Express
text: PayEm has integrated its global spend management and procurement platform with American Express. This integration allows American Express Corporate and Business Card Members to use their existing account and PayEm's platform to create and distribute on-demand virtual cards, PayEm said in a Wednesday (June 21) press release.

title: Enthusiasm for Tech Stocks and Optimism About Fed Rate Hikes Lift Markets
text: Investors' enthusiasm for tech stocks and optimism that the Fed will slow rate hikes lifts markets.

title: Why American Express Stock Was Volatile Today
text: An analyst from Citi lowered American Express's price target and put it on a negative catalyst watch. American Express remains a solid long-term stock, and is coming off a strong Q1.

title: Millionaires' Secret: Buy These 3 Reliable Blue-Chip Growth Stocks
text: Blue-chip stocks are securities of companies that have been around for a long time, have an established track record, are profitable and are viewed as financially fit. Blue-chip stocks tend to be more stable during a market downturn and they are often the first names to recover when conditions improve.

title: AmEx (AXP) Eyes Tie-Ups to Keep Up With the Digitization Trend
